title: LCBE_LAYOUT v1.0
description: Spatial and temporal scene layout passed between LCBE-ecosystem ComfyUI nodes (typically PAL → LCBE). Coordinate conventions are LOCKED for the lifetime of v1: right-handed, Y-up, metres, radians, vertical FOV, quaternions in [x, y, z, w] order, time in seconds, frames zero-indexed. A future consumer needing different conventions converts at the boundary — the wire format does not negotiate.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this payload. Consumers branch on this, not on socket type.

shot_id.description: LCBE shot ID if the layout originated from an LCBE shot. Absent for pre-LCBE PAL exports.

duration_seconds.description: Total shot duration in seconds. Derived from longest camera track if absent.

frame_rate.description: Authoring frame rate in frames per second. Defaults to 24 if absent.

cameras.description: Camera objects, each with its own keyframe track. May be empty; at least one camera is strongly recommended.

proxies.description: Proxy geometry representing actors, props, or scene elements that exist in the spatial layout but do not need full mesh data.

coordinate_system.description: Runtime self-check block. All four fields are constants in v1.0 — a consumer reading an unknown payload fails loudly if any value diverges, rather than silently misinterpreting data.

translation.description: World-space position as [x, y, z] in metres.

rotation_quaternion.description: Rotation as quaternion [x, y, z, w]. Matches Three.js convention. Note: Unity and Unreal use [w, x, y, z] — convert at the boundary, never in the wire format.

scale.description: Per-axis scale [sx, sy, sz] as unitless multipliers. Use [1, 1, 1] for unscaled.

id.description: Unique within the payload. Stable across re-exports.

fov_vertical_radians.description: Authoritative vertical field of view in radians. Matches Three.js PerspectiveCamera.fov after the engine's internal degrees→radians conversion.

focal_length_mm.description: Optional metadata. Must be consistent with fov_vertical_radians given sensor width. Formula: fov_vertical_radians = 2 * atan(sensor_height / (2 * focal_length)), with sensor_height = sensor_width * 24/36 for the default 36mm full-frame sensor.

near.description: Near clipping plane in metres. Defaults to 0.1.

far.description: Far clipping plane in metres. Defaults to 1000.

time_seconds.description: Time of this keyframe in seconds. Cameras do not scale, so no scale field is present.

kind.description: Proxy geometry kind. box: unit cube at origin, transformed. sphere: unit-radius sphere at origin. capsule: 1 m total height, 0.25 m radius, Y-axis aligned. mesh_ref: external mesh, requires mesh_ref field.

url.description: Resolvable LCBE asset URL. Must be either an lcbe://asset/{asset_id} reference or an absolute HTTPS URL to the LCBE asset proxy (/v1/media/asset/{asset_id}/{format}). Local-machine paths (file://, /Users/..., C:\...) and non-LCBE URLs are rejected by validation. Producers like PAL must upload local GLBs via POST /v1/media/asset before emitting payloads that reference them.

format.description: Asset format. v1.0 supports glb only. fbx and usd are reserved for v1.1+ additive expansion when an actual consumer needs them.

ground_plane.description: Ground plane convention. v1.0 supports y=0 only. Future versions may allow tilted planes.

scale_reference.description: Free-text human-readable scale hint (e.g. 'human_height_1.7m'). Aids consumers in sanity-checking scale.
